summary: Displaced midshaft clavicular fractures are routinely managed with surgery due to clear recognition in surgical classification systems through procedures like open reduction and internal fixation. These interventions are formally documented and classified under approved shoulder/arm reconstructive surgeries, supporting their use even when non-surgical options exist.
